---
title: "CV"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

A more complete CV can be found <a href="files/cv_rhodes.pdf" target="_blank">here</a>.

<br></br>

## Education and Training

+ Post-Doctoral Research Fellow: *The University of Missouri* (2016 -- present)
+ PhD Psychology: *The University of Edinburgh* (2012 -- 2016)
+ MSc Human Cognitive Neuropsychology: *The University of Edinburgh* (2011 -- 2012)
+ BSc (hons) Psychology: *The University of Leeds* (2008 -- 2011)

<br></br>

## Publications

Rhodes, S., Cowan, N., Parra, M. A., & Logie, R. H. (*in press*). Interaction Effects on Common Measures of Sensitivity: Choice of Measure, Type I Error and Power. *Behavior Research Methods*. <a href="https://link.springer.com/article/10.3758%2Fs13428-018-1081-0" target="_blank">[link]</a> <a href="files/Rhodes-et-al-BRM.pdf" target="_blank" style="color:teal">(pdf)</a> <a href="https://github.com/stephenrho/MeasuresAndErrors" target="_blank" style="color:firebrick">(data/code)</a>

Rhodes, S. & Cowan, N. (2018). Attention in Working Memory: Attention is Needed but it Yearns to be Free. *Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences, 1424*(1), 52-63. <a href="https://nyaspubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/nyas.13652" target="_blank">[link]</a> <a href="files/rhodes_cowan_nyas.pdf" target="_blank" style="color:teal">(pdf)</a>

Rhodes, S., Cowan, N., Hardman, K. O., & Logie, R. H. (2018). Informed Guessing in Change Detection. *Journal of Experimental Psychology: Learning, Memory, & Cognition, 44*(7), 1023-1035. <a href="https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/29265829" target="_blank">[link]</a> <a href="https://github.com/stephenrho/Guessing/blob/master/paper/Rhodes_et_al_Guessing.pdf" target="_blank" style="color:teal">(pdf)</a> <a href="https://github.com/stephenrho/Guessing" target="_blank" style="color:firebrick">(data/code)</a>

Rhodes, S., Parra, M. A., Cowan, N., & Logie, R. H. (2017). Healthy Aging and Visual Working Memory: The Role of Mixing Feature and Conjunction Changes. *Psychology & Aging, 32* (4), 354-366. <a href="http://psycnet.apa.org/journals/pag/32/4/354/" target="_blank">[link]</a> <a href="files/RhodesEtAl2017.pdf" target="_blank" style="color:teal">(pdf)</a> <a href="https://github.com/stephenrho/BindingData/tree/master/RhodesEtAl_PsychAging" target="_blank" style="color:firebrick">(data/code)</a>

Smith, K., Ricaud, B., Shahid, N., Rhodes, S., Starr, J. M., Ibanez, A., Parra, M. A., Escudero, J., & Vandergheynst, P. (2017). Locating Temporal Functional Dynamics of Visual Short-Term Memory Binding using Graph Modular Dirichlet Energy. *Scientific Reports, 7*, 42013. <a href="https://www.nature.com/articles/srep42013" target="_blank">[link]</a> <a href="files/SmithEtAl2017.pdf" target="_blank" style="color:teal">(pdf)</a>

Brown, L. A., Niven, E. H., Logie, R. H., Rhodes, S., & Allen, R. J. (2017). Visual Feature Binding in Younger and Older Adults: Encoding and Suffix Interference Effects. *Memory, 25* (2), 261-275. <a href="http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/09658211.2016.1156705" target="_blank">[link]</a> <a href="files/BrownEtAlMemory.pdf" target="_blank" style="color:teal">(pdf)</a> 

Rhodes, S., Parra, M. A., & Logie, R. H. (2016). Ageing and Feature Binding in Visual Working Memory: The Role of Presentation Time. *The Quarterly Journal of Experimental Psychology, 69* (4), 654-668. 
<a href="http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/17470218.2015.1038571" target="_blank">[link]</a> <a href="files/RhodesParraLogie2016.pdf" target="_blank" style="color:teal">(pdf)</a> <a href="https://github.com/stephenrho/BindingData/tree/master/RhodesEtAl_QJEP" target="_blank" style="color:firebrick">(data/code)</a>

Rhodes, S., & Parra, M. A. (2016). *Executive Functioning*. Encyclopedia of Geropsychology, Springer. <a href="http://link.springer.com/referencework/10.1007%2F978-981-287-080-3" target="_blank">[link]</a> <a href="files/RhodesAndParra_ExecutiveFunctioning.pdf" target="_blank" style="color:teal">(pdf)</a>

<br></br>

## In Progress

Morey, C., Rhodes, S. & Cowan, N. (*submitted*). Sensory-Motor Integration and Brain Lesions: Progress Toward Explaining Domain-Specific Phenomena Within Domain-General Working Memory.

Rhodes, S., Jaroslawska, A. J., Doherty, J. M., Barrouillet, P., Camos, V., Cowan, N., Naveh-Benjamin, M., & Logie, R. H. (*submitted*). Storage and Processing in Working Memory: Assessing Dual Task Performance and Task Prioritization Across the Adult Lifespan.

Doherty, J. M., Belletier, C., Rhodes, S., Jaroslawska, A., Camos, V., Barrouillet, P., Cowan, N., Naveh- Benjamin, M., & Logie, R. H. (*submitted*). Dual-Task Costs in Working Memory: An Adversarial Collaboration.

<br></br>

## Selected Posters and Presentations

Rhodes, S., Parra, M. A., Cowan, N., & Logie, R. H. (2016, May). *Healthy Ageing and Binding in Visual Working Memory: Mixed Versus Blocked Designs.* Poster presented at the International meeting of the Psychonomic Society, Granada, Spain. <a href="files/RhodesEtAlGranada.pdf" target="_blank" style="color:teal">(pdf)</a>

Rhodes, S., Parra, M. A., Cowan, N., & Logie, R. H. (2015, Nov). *Visual Working Memory and Change Detection: Reassessing Whole Display Interference.* Poster presented at the annual meeting of the Psychonomic Society, Chicago, Illinois. <a href="files/RhodesEtAlChicago.pdf" target="_blank" style="color:teal">(pdf)</a>

Rhodes, S., Parra, M. A., Cowan, N., & Logie, R. H. (2014, Nov). *The Effect of Age on Visual Working Memory for Features and Conjunctions.* Poster presented at the annual meeting of the Psychonomic Society, Long Beach, California. <a href="files/RhodesEtAlLongBeach.pdf" target="_blank" style="color:teal">(pdf)</a>

Rhodes, S., Parra, M. A., Cowan, N., & Logie, R. H. (2014, Sep). *Does Healthy Ageing Affect the Ability to Bind Features in Visual Working Memory?* Presented at the Seventh European Working Memory Symposium, Edinburgh.

Rhodes, S., Parra, M. A., Cowan, N., & Logie, R. H. (2014, Apr). *Is Feature Binding in Visual Working Memory Age Invariant?* Poster presented at the Cognitive Aging Conference 2014, Atlanta, Georgia. <a href="files/RhodesEtAlAtlanta.pdf" target="_blank" style="color:teal">(pdf)</a>
